institution: Missouri University of Science and Technology

ai_strategy_lead.name: Intelligent Systems Center
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: Missouri University of Science and Technology has a public AI posture anchored in Intelligent Systems Center. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: Missouri University of Science and Technology publishes AI policy or guidance with a cautious stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: Intelligent Systems Center
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at Missouri University of Science and Technology is evidenced through Intelligent Systems Center and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.description: Missouri University of Science and Technology publishes guidance or policy for responsible AI use, with attention to privacy, security, integrity, ethics, transparency and human oversight.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets institutional guardrails for classroom, research, staff and administrative use.

initiatives.title: The Mill GPU-enabled HPC cluster
initiatives.description: The Mill is Missouri S&T’s newest computational HPC cluster and discloses 34 GPUs across V100 and H100 nodes, with access for S&T students, staff, and faculty.
initiatives.significance: This closes the compute-capacity gap with a named cluster and public GPU count.

investment.description: The public source did not disclose total acquisition or operating cost; the HPCC page says general access is available without additional cost and priority access is available at additional cost.

collaborations.description: The Mill is accessible to Missouri S&T researchers and collaborators across the University of Missouri System.

initiatives.title: Spring 2024 generative AI classroom guidelines
initiatives.description: The Provost’s March 2024 GAI guidance directed instructors to clarify course-level expectations and allowed a spring 2024 transition period while students and faculty adjusted to generative AI.
initiatives.significance: This adds assessment and syllabus-level evidence rather than only general AI policy.
initiatives.policy_implications: Places course-level AI-use disclosure and permissions with instructors.

Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning program

computing_infrastructure.summary: Missouri S&T Research Support Solutions describes The Mill as a shared HPC cluster with 229 compute nodes, 15,200 compute cores, and 34 GPUs across V100 and H100 GPU nodes; the campus HPCC page states it is available to students, staff, and faculty without additional cost, with priority access available at additional cost.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and inclusive AI access
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: The cited AI posture includes ethics, privacy, access, public-good, bias, workforce or broad literacy considerations.

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: Missouri University of Science and Technology has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is Intelligent Systems Center, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.
Some AI initiatives did not disclose dollar amounts, user counts, or detailed implementation metrics.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.
After targeted Worker 3 searches, Missouri S&T still did not disclose enterprise AI platform seat counts or contract values.
The Mill discloses a GPU count, but public sources did not disclose a comprehensive AI-specific operating budget beyond normal HPC access and priority-access notes.
